title: The Full Customer Journey
description: Explore the complete end-to-end lifecycle of a customer from initial lead through active customer status.

sections.title: The Journey at a Glance
sections.content:
Every customer at NxN follows the same fundamental path. The journey is identical regardless of deal size, geography, or vertical market. Each stage has distinct activities, different people involved, and specific systems interactions.

Let's walk through it together.

sections.title: From Prospect to Lead
sections.content:
Lead Intake: A prospect is identified from an approved source—an inbound inquiry, referral, partner introduction, or prospecting list. They enter Salesforce as a Lead record with basic company and contact information.

Verification: The team confirms three things: the company exists, the contact is real and has authority to engage, and there's a legitimate business need. This is due diligence, not qualification—we're verifying we're pursuing real opportunities.

Qualification: The team now validates the match: Does the prospect's need fit our solution? Do they have budget? Is there a decision timeline? A qualified lead meets our criteria to move into a sales opportunity.

sections.title: Converting to an Opportunity
sections.content:
Conversion: This is critical. When a lead qualifies, it converts into the account hierarchy. The conversion logic determines whether this is a net-new customer, a new division within an existing account, or a partner-managed deal. Conversion creates the foundational account, subsidiary accounts, and contact records for the entire customer lifecycle.

Account & Contact Setup: After conversion, the account and contact records are confirmed and enriched. Account data includes company info, billing/shipping addresses, industry, and classification. Contact records capture roles, decision authority, and relationship history for every stakeholder involved.

sections.title: Moving Through the Deal
sections.content:
With the account structure in place, the sales team creates an opportunity on the correct account (the Entity Child Account that will deliver the service). The opportunity moves through defined stages:

1. Qualification — Confirming fit and readiness
2. Solution Definition — Understanding and designing the customer solution
3. Pricing & Quotation — Preparing a detailed quote
4. Customer Review — Customer evaluation and feedback
5. Contracting — Legal review and terms finalization
6. Closed Won / Closed Lost — Deal closure

Each stage requires specific activities and gate criteria before advancing.

sections.title: Documentation & Approval
sections.content:
Quote: During Pricing & Quotation, the sales team creates a quote documenting products, services, quantities, pricing, terms, and commercial conditions. Internal approval is required before sending to the customer.

Contract: As closure approaches, legal and contracting teams create a purchase agreement with binding terms, conditions, warranty, support, pricing, and obligations. Authorized signatories on both sides execute the contract.

sections.title: From Closure to Customer
sections.content:
Closed Won / Closed Lost: The opportunity closes as Won (customer signed or provided acceptance) or Lost (competitor selected, customer deferred, or deal stalled). Won deals move to customer lifecycle management. Lost deals are analyzed for win/loss patterns.

Active Customer Lifecycle: After Closed Won, the account enters Active status. Account managers own the relationship, monitor usage and satisfaction, manage renewals, and identify expansion opportunities. The account stays Active as long as the commercial relationship continues.

Dormant & Blocked: If an active commitment ends (non-renewal or service discontinuation), the account moves to Dormant—monitored for reactivation. If a billing, legal, or compliance issue blocks normal business, the account moves to Blocked until resolved.

sections.title: How It Works in Salesforce
sections.content:
This entire journey lives in Salesforce through Lead, Account, Contact, Opportunity, Quote, and Contract objects. Each has specific fields, stages, and governance rules.

Understanding how each stage connects and what must be completed at each step is the foundation of using Salesforce effectively at NxN.

keyTakeaways: Lead Intake through Closed Won follows a defined sequence with distinct stages for verification, qualification, and conversion
Account hierarchy (Parent and Entity Child) must be established correctly during conversion to ensure proper revenue attribution
Each stage requires specific activities and completion before moving forward
